name: Teamhair
description: Teamhair, also known as the Hill of Tara, is an ancient ceremonial and burial site located in County Meath, Ireland. It holds immense significance in Irish mythology and history, serving as the traditional seat of the High Kings of Ireland. According to legend, the Lia Fáil, the Stone of Destiny, resided at Teamhair, and roared when a true king stood upon it. The site was a focal point for political and religious gatherings, and its earthworks and monuments bear witness to centuries of human activity and spiritual importance. Teamhair is associated with numerous mythological figures and events, representing a powerful connection to the ancient past and the ancestral spirits of Ireland. It was a sacred space where rituals were performed, laws were proclaimed, and the fate of the kingdom was decided. The landscape itself is imbued with meaning, offering panoramic views of the surrounding countryside and connecting the earthly realm with the celestial. Over time, Teamhair has become a symbol of Irish identity, sovereignty, and cultural heritage, continuing to inspire awe and reverence in those who visit its hallowed grounds. Its enduring presence serves as a reminder of the rich tapestry of Celtic mythology and the enduring power of the past.
subtitle: Seat of the High Kings of Ireland

keyMyths.title: The Stone of Destiny
keyMyths.description: The Lia Fáil, or Stone of Destiny, was said to roar when a rightful king stood upon it during his inauguration. This act signified his divine right to rule and legitimized his claim to the throne.

keyMyths.title: The Feast of Tara
keyMyths.description: The Feast of Tara was a legendary event held at Teamhair, where the High King and his nobles gathered for a grand celebration. It was a time for feasting, storytelling, and the reaffirmation of royal authority.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Comprehensive guide to Irish mythological figures and places.
sources.text: Comprehensive guide to Irish mythological figures and places.
